mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 R621H mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 R621H demonstrate this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

In vitro studies demonstrate that this mutation is resistant to the FGFR inhibitor BGJ398 when expressed in TEL-FGFR3 fusion-positive murine fibroblasts as measured by sustained proliferation upon drug treatment (PMID: 28034880).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3-TACC3 fusion involves the N-terminus of FGFR3, including the FGFR3 extracellular and kinase domains, fused to the C-terminus of TACC3, including the TACC3 coiled-coil domain. This mutation has been found in glioma. Expression of this mutation in a rat fibroblast cell line and murine astrocytes demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased anchorage-independent growth, chromosome segregation defects and formation of in vivo brain tumors compared to vector control and wildtype FGFR3 and TACC3 (PMID: 22837387). In vitro and in vivo studies demonstrate that this mutation is sensitive to the FGFR3 tyrosine kinase inhibitor erdafitinib when expressed in glioma cells or mouse astrocytes as measured by decreased cell proliferation and tumor growth upon drug treatment (PMID: 25609060). Two patients with glioma harboring this mutation demonstrated clinical responses to erdafitinib (PMID: 25609060). A patient with gastric cancer harboring an FGFR3–TACC3 fusion had a confirmed response to the highly selective, irreversible FGFR1–4 inhibitor futibatinib (PMID 34551969). In the Phase II FIGHT-207 trial examining pemigatinib treatment in patients harboring FGFR3-TACC, four patients with central nervous system tumors and two patients with non-small cell lung cancer demonstrated progressive disease, two patients with central nervous system tumors and one patient with non-small cell lung cancer demonstrated stable disease, one patient with a central nervous system tumor demonstrated partial response and one patient with a central nervous system tumor demonstrated complete response  (Abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023. https://aacrjournals.org/cancerres/article/83/8_Supplement/CT016/725419).

consequence.description: NA

mutationEffectAbstracts.abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023.

Expression of this variant in an embryonic kidney cell line demonstrated that it is activating, as measured by increased activation in the absence of ligand compared to wildtype (PMID: 23437153).

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 M528I mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. This mutation has been found as a germline variant that segregates with proportionate short stature. Expression of this mutation in rat chondrosarcoma chondrocytes, embryonic kidney and murine fibroblast cell lines demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased pathway activation compared to wildtype (PMID: 25777271).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 K508M mutation is located in the extracellular domain of the protein. While this mutation has only been studied in the context of an FGFR3 fusion, expression of this mutation in an FGFR3-TACC3 positive-rat fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is inactivating as measured by decreased protein activity and colony formation compared to FGFR3-TACC3 alone (PMID: 22837387).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 S249C mutation is located in the extracellular domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in bladder cancer (PMID: 28572459). Expression of this mutation in murine fibroblast and bladder cell lines demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein and pathway activation, colony formation, and tumor formation in a xenograft model compared to wildtype (PMID: 16338952, 19749790). In the Phase II FIGHT-207 trial examining pemigatinib treatment with patients harboring FGFR3 S249C, three patients with bladder cancer and one patient with cancer of unknown primary demonstrated progressive disease, and four patients with bladder cancer and one patient with cancer of unknown primary demonstrated stable disease (Abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023. https://aacrjournals.org/cancerres/article/83/8_Supplement/CT016/725419). In a Phase I trial, two patients with urothelial carcinoma harboring FGFR3 S249C were treated with futibatinib and demonstrated partial response with progression-free survivals of 2.7 months and 4.7 months, respectively (PMID: 34551969).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectAbstracts.abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023.

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 G802_X807del stop-loss mutation likely extends the FGFR3 protein, disrupting the inhibitory function of the C-terminal domain with the addition of an alpha helix that may act as a second transmembrane domain or inhibit glycosylation of FGFR3 (PMID: 26003532, 7647778, 17320202). FGFR3 stop-loss mutations are associated with thanatophoric dysplasia type I, but have also been seen in cancer (PMID: 7647778, 8845844, 36639200, 35819403; GENIE Cohort v19.0, accessed 03/24/26). While FGFR3 G802_X807del has not been functionally characterized, similar mutations that also result in read-through of the FGFR3 stop codon have demonstrated gain-of-function activity (PMID: 36639200). Expression of FGFR3 *807Rext*141 in a HEK cell line demonstrated increased tyrosine-phosphorylated intracellular puncta as measured by phosphotyrosine immunoprecipitation and Western blot assays compared to wildtype FGFR3 (PMID: 36639200). A patient with multiple myeloma and t(4;14) who harbored a subclonal FGFR3 G802_X807del mutation demonstrated loss of FGFR3 G802_X807del-containing cells upon treatment with erdafitinib, though did not demonstrate a clinical response to erdafitinib (PMID: 36639200).

consequence.description: An inframe non synonymous variant that deletes bases from the coding sequence

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 G66fs*32 mutation is located in the Ig-like C2-type I domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 G66fs*32 demonstrated this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, because the number of nucleotides inserted or deleted is not a multiple of three

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 G375C mutation is located in the transmembrane domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in skeletal disorders. mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3*807Rext*141 stop-loss mutation extends the FGFR3 protein by 141 amino acids, disrupting the inhibitory function of the C-terminal domain with the addition of an alpha helix that may act as a second transmembrane domain or inhibit glycosylation of FGFR3 (PMID: 26003532, 7647778, 17320202). FGFR3 stop-loss mutations are associated with thanatophoric dysplasia type I, but have also been seen in cancer (PMID: 7647778, 8845844, 36639200, 35819403; GENIE Cohort v19.0, accessed 03/24/26). In a HEK cell line demonstrated increased tyrosine-phosphorylated intracellular puncta as measured by phosphotyrosine immunoprecipitation and Western blot assays compared to wildtype FGFR3 (PMID: 36639200). While FGFR3 *807Wext*141 has not been documented in clinical studies, a patient with multiple myeloma and t(4;14) who harbored a subclonal stop-loss mutation, FGFR3 G802_X807del, demonstrated loss of FGFR3 G802_X807del-containing cells upon treatment with erdafitinib, though did not demonstrate a clinical response to erdafitinib (PMID: 36639200).

In vitro studies with MCF10A cells expressing FGFR3-ELAVL3 demonstrate that the fusion is activating as measured by increased colony formation and upregulation of ERK1/2 phosphorylation compared to wildtype (PMID: 35138907).

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 K650E mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein (PMID: 19749790). This mutation has been found in multiple myeloma (PMID: 28572459). Expression of this mutation in murine fibroblast, multiple myeloma and epithelial cell lines demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein and pathway activation, cell proliferation and colony formation compared to wildtype (PMID: 11429702, 19749790, 11429702). In vitro studies demonstrate that this mutation is resistant to the FGFR inhibitor BGH398 when present in a TEL-FGFR3 Fusion-expressing murine B-cell line as measured by the sustained cell proliferation following drug treatment (PMID: 28034880).

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 L608V mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in transitional cell carcinoma (PMID: 28572459). consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 G380R mutation is located in the transmembrane domain of the protein and is a hotspot residue. This mutation has been found as a germline mutation in dwarfism (PMID: 10611230). There is conflicting functional data for this mutation. Expression of this mutation in vitro, in embryonic kidney and rat mesenchymal stem cell lines and in a transgenic mouse model demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein activity and protein accumulation in mouse growth plates compared to wildtype (PMID: 10611230, 21324899, 12461689). However, expression of this mutation in a chondrocyte cell line demonstrated that it is inactivating as measured by decreased proliferation and differentiation compared to wildtype (PMID: 10646125).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 *807Wext*101 stop-loss mutation extends the FGFR3 protein by 101 amino acids, disrupting the inhibitory function of the C-terminal domain with the addition of an alpha helix that may act as a second transmembrane domain or inhibit glycosylation of FGFR3 (PMID: 26003532, 7647778, 17320202). FGFR3 stop-loss mutations are associated with thanatophoric dysplasia type I, but have also been seen in cancer (PMID: 7647778, 8845844, 36639200, 35819403, 28572459)(GENIE Cohort v18.0, accessed 01/26/26). While FGFR3 *807Wext*101 has not been functionally characterized, similar mutations that also result in read-through of the FGFR3 stop codon have demonstrated gain-of-function activity (PMID: 36639200). Expression of FGFR3 *807Rext*141 in a HEK cell line demonstrated increased tyrosine-phosphorylated intracellular puncta as measured by phosphotyrosine immunoprecipitation and Western blot assays compared to wildtype FGFR3 (PMID: 36639200). A patient with multiple myeloma and t(4;14) who harbored a subclonal stop-loss mutation, FGFR3 G802_X807del, demonstrated loss of FGFR3 G802_X807del-containing cells upon treatment with erdafitinib, though did not demonstrate a clinical response to erdafitinib (PMID: 36639200).

consequence.description: A sequence variant where at least one base of the terminator codon (stop) is changed, resulting in an elongated transcript

Expression of this mutation in a murine fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is neutral as measured by no change in protein activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 26992226). However, expression of this mutation in vitro demonstrated that it is likely activating as measured by increased protein stability (PMID: 29478821).

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 R248C mutation is located in the extracellular domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in bladder cancer (PMID: 28572459). Expression of this mutant in a murine B-cell line demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased constitutive protein activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 8640234).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 D646Y mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in urinary tract cancer. Expression of this mutation in a murine fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is neutral as measured by no change in protein activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 26992226)

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 R603Q mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 R603Q demonstrate this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 G382D mutation is located in the transmembrane domain of the protein. There is conflicting functional data for this mutation. Expression of this variant in an embryonic kidney cell line demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein and pathway activation compared to wildtype. However, expression of this mutation in a murine fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is likely neutral as measured by no change in colony formation compared to wildtype (PMID: 11429702).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 N540K mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. This mutation has been found as a germline mutation in hypochondroplasia (PMID: 21225389). Expression of this mutation in a murine fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 26992226).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3-BAIAP2L1 fusion involves the N-terminus of FGFR3 fused to the C-terminus of BAIAP2L1. Expression of this mutation in a murine fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is activating, as measured by increased protein activation and anchorage-independent growth compared to wildtype (PMID: 23175443).

consequence.description: NA

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR2 R248_S249insC mutation is located in the extracellular domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR2 R248_S249insC demonstrated this mutation is activating as measured by increased transformation activity and proliferation compared to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: An inframe non synonymous variant that inserts bases into in the coding sequence

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 P716H mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 P716H demonstrate this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 E627D mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in urinary tract cancer. Expression of this mutation in a murine fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is neutral as measured by no change in protein activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 26992226)

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR2 A391V mutation is located in the transmembrane domain of the protein. There is conflicting functional data for this variant. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR2 A391V demonstrate this mutation is activating as measured by increased transformation activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 34272467). However, in vitro studies with MCF10A cells demonstrate that this mutation is neutral as measured by similar cellular proliferation and viability as compared to wildtype (PMID: 29533785).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 Y373C mutation is located in the transmembrane domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in bladder cancer (PMID: 28572459). Expression of this mutation in murine fibroblast and bladder cell lines demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein activation, colony formation and tumor formation in a xenograft model compared to wildtype (PMID: 16338952, 11429702, 19749790). In the Phase II FIGHT-207 trial examining pemigatinib treatment with patients harboring FGFR3 Y373C, three patients with bladder cancer demonstrated progressive disease, stable disease and partial response with a progression-free survival of 3 months, 5 months and 10 months, respectively (Abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023. https://aacrjournals.org/cancerres/article/83/8_Supplement/CT016/725419).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectAbstracts.abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023.

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 A257V mutation is located in the Ig-like C2-type III domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 A257V demonstrated this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 K650Q mutation is located in the extracellular domain of the protein (PMID: 19749790). This mutation has been found as a germline mutation in acanthosis nigricans (PMID: 18000903). Expression of this mutation in vitro demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 11055896).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 E322K mutation is located in the Ig-like C2-type III domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 E322K demonstrated this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 T311fs*55 mutation is located in the Ig-like C2-type III domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 T311fs*55 demonstrated this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, because the number of nucleotides inserted or deleted is not a multiple of three

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 K403fs*93 mutation is located in the cytoplasmic domain of the protein. In vitro studies with Ba/F3 cells expressing FGFR3 K403fs*93  demonstrate this mutation is likely neutral as measured by transformation activity and proliferation comparable to wildtype (PMID: 34272467).

consequence.description: A sequence variant which causes a disruption of the translational reading frame, because the number of nucleotides inserted or deleted is not a multiple of three

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3 E466K mutation is located in the kinase domain of the protein. This mutation has been found in urinary tract cancer (PMID: 26992226). There is conflicting functional information for this mutation. Expression of this mutation in a murine fibroblast cell line demonstrated that it is neutral as measured by no change in protein activity compared to wildtype (PMID: 26992226). However, expression of this mutation in vitro demonstrated that it is activating as measured by increased protein stability (PMID: 29478821)

consequence.description: A sequence variant, that changes one or more bases, resulting in a different amino acid sequence but where the length is preserved

mutationEffectDescription: The FGFR3-RGS12 fusion is a fusion of the N-terminal end of the FGFR1 gene with the C-terminal end of the RGS12 gene. Fusions of FGFR3 have been shown to be oncogenic, and therefore the FGFR3-RGS12 fusion is considered likely oncogenic (PMID: 23661334, 23558953, 22837387). In a Phase II trial, a patient with cancer of unknown primary harboring FGFR3-RGS12 was treated with pemigatnib and demonstrated progressive disease (Abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023. https://aacrjournals.org/cancerres/article/83/8_Supplement/CT016/725419).

consequence.description: NA

mutationEffectAbstracts.abstract: Rodon et al. Abstract# CT016. AACR 2023.
